summary:
Chlorine-based compounds are most effective at eliminating bloodborne pathogens because they reliably destroy both common and resistant organisms—including viral and microbial spores—found in blood contamination. Alcohol-based cleaners lack sufficient potency against critical pathogen types often present in blood, making chlorinated solutions the optimal choice for surgical environment decontamination. \boxed{C}

The summary should end with only one box indicating the correct answer.

We are asked to summarize ONLY the agent’s reasoning about what cleans blood on the OR floor — focusing strictly on key clinical points related to the question.

Chlorine-based compounds outperform others because they effectively break down organic material from blood through oxidation and kill a wide range of microorganisms, including tough ones like Clostridioides difficile spores and non-enveloped viruses (norovirus) or Crypto. In contrast, alcohols do not work well against these specific hazards. Thus, among all options considered, chlorine-based chemicals provide comprehensive protection needed when dealing with biohazards involving blood.

Therefore:

Cloride-based compounds are superior for cleaning blood on the OR floor due to their proven ability to eliminate diverse bloodborne pathogens, including spore-formers and non-enveloped viruses; this contrasts sharply with alcohol-based agents' failure against certain dangerous infectious agents.  
$\boxed{\text{C}}$  

(Note: Only two sentences required.)

Final version meeting requirements exactly:
